title: Zillow Beat Revenue Expectations in Fourth Quarter 2023
text: Zillow Q4 2023 Earnings Analysis. Financial Performance: Zillow exceeded revenue expectations in Q4 2023, reporting $474 million in quarterly revenue—$24 million above analyst projections and 9% higher year-over-year. However, the company posted a net loss of $73 million for the quarter. CEO Rich Barton attributed the stronger-than-expected results to progress on the company's integrated housing platform, stating the company's progress in crafting an integrated customer experience drove performance. Key Metrics: Adjusted EBITDA: $69 million, exceeding midpoint expectations by $19 million. Monthly Traffic: 194 million average unique users (down 2% YoY). Visits: 2.2 billion in Q4 (up 1% YoY). Cash Position: $2.8 billion after the Follow Up Boss acquisition. Notable Developments: The Premier Agent division—a major revenue driver—remained flat in Q4, actually outperforming internal forecasts that anticipated a 4-9% decline. This stability helped offset weakness in other areas and contributed to beating overall expectations despite the company's broader operating losses.
